Industry news

Baking Paper Packaging News

Daily buyer-focused updates on greaseproof paper, baking cups, air fryer paper liners, food-contact paper packaging, PFAS-free materials and EUDR compliance.

Ask for Product SupportRead Buyer Guides

Market updates

Latest Food Paper Packaging Updates

2026-06-26PFAS testing

1. New organic-fluorine screening method sharpens PPWR evidence planning

Norner has introduced a Total Organic Fluorine screening method using pyrolysis-GC/MS to distinguish PFAS-relevant organic fluorine from inorganic fluorine in solid materials. The method is positioned for coatings, composites and products with uncertain PFAS histories, with targeted PFAS analysis added where the screening result or risk profile requires it.

Source: Packaging Insights
2026-06-23Food-contact compliance

2. Official guidance puts the EU and Northern Ireland BPA deadline into buyer terms

Food Standards Scotland says most newly manufactured or imported food-contact materials first placed on the EU or Northern Ireland market from 20 July 2026 must meet the EU restrictions on intentionally used BPA and other hazardous bisphenols, subject to defined transitions. It also distinguishes this requirement from the current position in Great Britain.

Source: Food Standards Scotland
2026-06-23Bakery packaging trends

3. Carrefour links paper and bakery packaging changes to cost and EPR pressure

Carrefour plans to remove 5,000 tonnes of plastic packaging through measures covering multipacks, refills, paper formats and bakery packaging. The retailer says packaging savings will support lower prices, showing how material reduction and EPR charges are becoming commercial purchasing factors as well as sustainability targets.

Source: Packaging Insights
2026-06-15PFAS-free paper

4. EU guidance clarifies the August PFAS checkpoint for food-contact packaging

New European Commission guidance explains a stepwise testing approach for the PPWR's PFAS limits and confirms that food-contact packaging placed on the EU market from 12 August 2026 must comply. The guidance also distinguishes stock already placed on the market from new supply.

Source: Packaging Europe
2026-06-05Low-odor printing

5. India food authority warning highlights migration risks from non-food-grade printing

India's food safety authority has again directed food businesses to stop using newspapers to pack or serve food, citing possible migration of printing-ink components and contaminants. The warning is a practical reminder that printed paper must be designed and documented for its intended food-contact use.

Source: Press Information Bureau, Government of India
2026-05-28Cupcake liners

6. Taller cupcake liners emerge as a simple bakery presentation upgrade

Consumer baking coverage is drawing attention to taller paper liners as a low-cost way to improve cupcake height, shape and shelf presence. For commercial buyers, the trend turns small dimensional differences into a visible part of the finished product.

Source: Good Housekeeping
2026-05-04EUDR

7. European Commission simplifies EUDR implementation while keeping buyer traceability relevant

The Commission's 2026 EUDR review packages updated guidance, FAQs, product-scope work and information-system changes. The simplification effort is expected to reduce compliance costs, but paper and packaging buyers still need clear product-scope decisions and dependable supplier-origin records.

Source: European Commission
2026-04-10BfR compliance

8. BfR draft consolidates food-contact paper recommendations and sharpens substance identification

Germany's BfR is consulting on a consolidated, tabular revision of Recommendations XXXVI, XXXVI/1, XXXVI/2 and XXXVI/3 for food-contact paper and board. The draft adds CAS-number precision, which can affect how raw materials are matched to compliant entries.

Source: German Federal Institute for Risk Assessment (BfR)
2026-04-10Air fryer liners

9. Air fryer paper liner outlook keeps sizing, heat safety and private label in focus

A 2026 market outlook links continued paper-liner demand to air-fryer adoption, convenience cooking and commercial-kitchen use. Product development is concentrating on basket fit, grease resistance, heat performance, compostability claims and retail-ready private-label formats.

Source: IndexBox
2026-04-10Greaseproof paper

10. PFAS-free grease-resistant paper targets demanding foodservice applications

Koehler Paper has introduced NexPlus OGR for burgers, fries, wraps and other oily foods, combining a grease-and-oil barrier made without fluorochemicals with recyclability. The launch reflects foodservice demand for paper formats that can meet both functional and circularity requirements.

Source: Koehler Group
